First release of the Rust implementation of Netflix Dynomite, a thin
distributed replication layer for Redis and Memcached datastores.

This release lands all 16 staged deliverables tracked in `PLAN.md`.

## What's in the box

- `dynomite` library crate: embeddable engine. `forbid(unsafe_code)`
  workspace-wide.
- `dynomited` binary crate: server with full CLI, daemonize, pidfile,
  signal handling, manpage.
- `dyn-hash-tool` utility: ports `dyn_hash_tool.c` plus a `--c-compat`
  mode for byte-equivalent output.
- `crates/fuzz/`: 7 cargo-fuzz targets (Redis req+rsp parsers,
  Memcache req+rsp parsers, DNODE parser, conf parser, AES decrypt)
  with 63 hand-curated seeds.

## Stages delivered

| Stage | Subsystem |
|---|---|
| 0 | workspace, flake, CI, docs scaffolding |
| 1 | core: types, log, signal, task, ring queue, dict |
| 2 | io: mbuf, cbuf, reactor, Transport trait |
| 3 | hashkit: 13 algorithms, DynToken, ketama, modula |
| 4 | conf: YAML schema; every bundled fixture round-trips |
| 5 | stats: 79 metrics, histogram, REST snapshots |
| 6 | crypto: AES-128-CBC + RSA OAEP via pure-Rust RustCrypto |
| 7 | msg + DNODE codec (179 MsgType variants) |
| 8 | Redis + Memcache parsers + 10 byte-identical Lua repair scripts |
| 9 | transports + 6 conn FSMs + QUIC via `quiche` |
| 10 | cluster: gossip, dispatch, snitch, vnode, 3 seeds providers |
| 11 | entropy reconciliation |
| 12 | dynomited binary: CLI, daemonize, pidfile, run loop, manpage |
| 13 | embedding API (SemVer-hardened public surface) |
| 14 | conformance + regression suites + Python harness port |
| 15 | 7 fuzz targets, 7 micro benches, macro bench scaffold, coverage gate |
| 16 | 1-hour chaos test, dual-platform CI, distribution packaging |

## Test surface at v0.1.0

- 607 nextest tests (default features)
- 664 nextest tests (`--all-features`)
- 566 doctests
- 24 `hegeltest` property tests (5 at 1024-case soak budget)
- 7 `cargo-fuzz` targets with 63 hand-curated seeds (including the
  verbatim `dyn_test.c` blob)
- 50 integration conformance tests gated behind `--features integration`
- 1-hour chaos test (`--features chaos`) with configurable
  `CHAOS_DURATION_SECS` for smoke runs
- 7 `criterion` micro benchmark files + 1 macro bench scaffold

## Documentation

The mdBook reference manual lives under `docs/book/`:

- Architecture, configuration, protocols (Redis / Memcache / DNODE)
- Transports (TCP, QUIC), security
- Embedding API guide and examples
- Operations: conformance, benchmarks, coverage, chaos, release runbook
- Per-stage journals under `docs/journal/`
- Live C-to-Rust symbol map at `docs/parity.md`

## Acknowledgement

This Rust implementation is a port of the original Netflix Dynomite
project (<https://github.com/Netflix/dynomite>), which itself
extended Twitter's twemproxy. Both projects are licensed under the
Apache License 2.0; their notices are preserved in `NOTICE`.

## Known follow-ups

- Coverage at 77.44% line / 78.66% region / 77.88% function. The 95%
  target is tracked in `docs/coverage-deviations.md` (89 module rows);
  the chaos test exercises the listener/conn-FSM modules and is
  expected to lift those into compliance on a production run.
- `dynomited` cross-process accept loop currently logs a warning and
  closes connections; cross-process traffic is the responsibility of
  the binary's full path (Stage 9 conn FSM); the embedded API path
  via `inject_request` is the supported in-process surface for v0.1.
- A multi-hour chaos run + report curation into
  `dist/chaos-reports/v0.1.0/` is planned post-release.
